rationale.R1.6: The delegation asks only to look at a paragraph, making sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, flow, and register the central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified and does not explicitly request factual verification or rewriting.
rationale.RX.2: The paragraph contains strong deployment and novelty claims, so avoiding overstatement is an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection and paragraph-level inspection rather than executing edits or broader review.
rationale.RX.4: The artifact invokes clinical and legal deployment contexts, so attention to potential downstream harm is always active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact without adding unsupported assumptions.
